description:
Implement the participant's `merge` function for the
external.github_issues.* projection overlap and the conformance tests
that lock the projection contract. Two layers:

1. external.github_issues.* overlap: both sides moved the foreign
   key under the issues plugin's own namespace. The plugin owns this
   projection authoritatively; merge picks the plugin's view (which
   is GH's view, since this projection mirrors GH). This is the
   default merge for an owned projection per SPEC §5.

2. task.status overlap when close_mirror == "authoritative": the
   plugin claims write-authority on status for the close transition
   *only*. The merge rule: GH's closed beats balls's open (mirrors
   the close); balls's any-state beats GH's open (balls is the
   workflow source of truth for non-close transitions). Conformance
   test: state round-trips through balls without GH ever opening a
   closed task back up.

WHY structuring it this way: SPEC §5 says "Plugins inherit a default
merge that picks the participant's view (since by §3, a participant
authoritatively owns its projection)". For projection-overlap that's
clean. The status-overlap is the deliberately-allowed special case;
making the asymmetry explicit (GH-closed wins; balls non-close wins)
prevents the operator's task being silently re-opened by an external
stale view.

ACCEPTANCE:
- merge() function in src/merge.rs, exported via the participant
  protocol's native describe handshake.
- Conformance tests:
  * Disjoint projections compose (external.github_issues set on the
    pull side, status set on the balls side, no conflict).
  * Overlapping external.github_issues.* converges on the plugin's
    view.
  * Status close-mirror: GH closed + balls open → balls closes.
  * Status close-mirror disabled: GH closed + balls open → conflict
    surfaced (not silently overridden).
  * Status non-close: GH open + balls in_progress → balls wins
    (operator workflow protected).
- 100% coverage; under 300 lines per file.
title: Conflict/merge: projection rules, status-overlap policy gating
